skill.name: activecampaign-automation
skill.description: Automates ActiveCampaign contact, tag, list, automation, and task workflows through Rube MCP and Composio tools.
skill.summary: Use Rube MCP to manage ActiveCampaign contacts, tags, subscriptions, automations, and tasks.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static external command, script, weak crypto, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from Markdown backticks, prose, and field names in SKILL.md. The real risk is legitimate but sensitive third-party MCP use that can read and modify ActiveCampaign CRM data.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Access to ActiveCampaign Data
medium_findings.description: The skill requires Rube MCP and an ActiveCampaign connection. This is intended, but it routes CRM operations through a third-party MCP service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The prerequisite and setup sections explicitly require Rube MCP and ActiveCampaign authentication. This confirms network and CRM data access, but not malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Workflow Instructions Can Mutate CRM Records
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to create contacts, change tags, alter list subscriptions, enroll automations, and create tasks. These actions can change customer records.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented workflows and quick reference list multiple state-changing ActiveCampaign operations. The risk is operational misuse, not hidden code execution.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Execution Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick alerts are Markdown formatting around tool names, parameters, and examples. SKILL.md contains instructions, not executable shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows inline code spans and fenced documentation examples. I did not find evidence of actual Ruby, shell, or JavaScript execution.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is Configuration Guidance
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to the Rube MCP server that the skill requires. Users should verify the endpoint before connecting accounts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is presented as setup documentation for a required MCP service. It is still relevant because it directs users to a third-party network endpoint.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: The static weak crypto and reconnaissance matches are incidental words in prose or field names. No cryptographic API, system scanning, or network scanning code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are metadata, task fields, and quick reference rows. They do not contain code paths or commands that perform crypto or reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: ActiveCampaign work often requires careful contact lookup, tagging, subscription, automation, and task steps. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured Rube MCP workflow for those tasks.
actual_capabilities: Find ActiveCampaign contacts by email, ID, or phone using Rube MCP tools.
Create contacts with email, name, phone, organization, job title, and tags.
Add or remove contact tags with documented action values and parameter rules.
Subscribe or unsubscribe contacts from lists when list IDs are known.
Enroll existing contacts in ActiveCampaign automations by automation ID.
Create contact tasks with due dates, task types, assignees, and notes.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active ActiveCampaign connection before use.
Does not create ActiveCampaign automations or discover every account setting.
List, automation, task type, and user IDs often must come from the ActiveCampaign UI.
Bulk operations need pacing and retry handling for ActiveCampaign rate limits.

use_cases.title: Prepare New Lead Records
use_cases.description: Create or update lead contacts, apply launch tags, and assign follow-up tasks after form submissions.

use_cases.title: Maintain Customer Segments
use_cases.description: Find contacts, adjust tags, and change list subscriptions during campaign cleanup or lifecycle updates.

use_cases.title: Trigger Sales Follow-Up
use_cases.description: Enroll qualified contacts in automations and create dated tasks for account owners.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Contact
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Rube MCP to search ActiveCampaign for this email: {{email}}. Return the matching contact ID and key profile fields.

prompt_templates.title: Create or Update a Lead
prompt_templates.prompt: Find or create an ActiveCampaign contact for {{email}} with name {{name}}. Apply these tags: {{tags}}.

prompt_templates.title: Manage Campaign Subscription
prompt_templates.prompt: Find {{email}}, confirm the contact ID, then {{subscribe_or_unsubscribe}} the contact from list ID {{list_id}}.

prompt_templates.title: Enroll and Assign Follow-Up
prompt_templates.prompt: Find {{email}}, enroll the contact in automation {{automation_id}}, and create a follow-up task due {{due_datetime}} for assignee {{assignee_id}}.

output_examples.input: Find jane@example.com and add the Webinar Lead tag.
output_examples.output: The contact was found by email. The Webinar Lead tag was added, and the contact ID was reported for audit tracking.

output_examples.input: Subscribe alex@example.com to list 12 and create a follow-up task for Friday.
output_examples.output: The contact lookup completed, list 12 was updated, and a dated follow-up task was created with the provided task type.

output_examples.input: Enroll sam@example.com in automation 44 after confirming the contact exists.
output_examples.output: The contact was verified first. The automation enrollment step then used the confirmed contact email and automation ID.

best_practices: Call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first so the current tool schemas drive every request.
Resolve contact, list, automation, task type, and assignee IDs before state-changing actions.
Confirm user intent before subscribing, unsubscribing, tagging, or enrolling contacts.
anti_patterns: Do not guess ActiveCampaign IDs from names or campaign titles.
Do not run bulk updates without rate limiting and rollback notes.
Do not create contacts or subscriptions without consent and compliance checks.

faq.question: Does this skill require an MCP server?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires Rube MCP and an active ActiveCampaign connection through RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS.

faq.question: Can it create ActiveCampaign automations?
faq.answer: No. It can enroll contacts in existing automations, but automation creation happens in ActiveCampaign.

faq.question: Can it find list IDs automatically?
faq.answer: Not always. The skill says list IDs may need to come from the ActiveCampaign UI.

faq.question: Can it process many contacts at once?
faq.answer: It can guide bulk tagging patterns, but batch work should use delays and handle rate limits.

faq.question: What data does it handle?
faq.answer: It handles CRM data such as email addresses, phone numbers, names, tags, list status, and tasks.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes, with a medium-risk warning because it connects to a third-party MCP service and changes CRM records.
